content: We visited communities around Harare to plant indigenous trees and run eco-awareness workshops for local schools. Big thanks to volunteers and partners — #MSUEnviro #GreenZimbabwe
Our students hosted the annual MSU Hack-A-Thon where teams developed drones for precision agriculture. Congratulations to winners from the Faculty of Engineering!
Hosted a public seminar on civic engagement and youth leadership in Zimbabwe. Panels included local MPs and civil society leaders.
A three-day engagement where students ran health clinics and digital literacy sessions for rural communities in Midlands province.
Showcased student artwork inspired by Zimbabwean heritage and resilience. Visitors from across Bulawayo attended.
A hands-on workshop on machine learning for agriculture — practical sessions on building predictive models using local datasets.
